text: So after laying the patient in supine position, draping and covering the skin, a longitudinal 3 cm incision was performed just over the xiphoid process.
Then removal of the xiphoid and applying of wound protector.
We started to remove some of the pericardial fat below the xiphoid in order to facilitate the exposure.
then the plural was bilaterally opened using the termy once the plural was opened bilaterally
this allows the mediastinum to move posteriorly providing better space for working the thymic tissue then carefully shaved from the pericardium

Adhesions between the thymus and the lung also removed carefully using the diathermy Applying some tension on the thymic horn during excision is important in order to remove as much as possible of the thymic tissue
During exposure of the innominate vein, it's crucial to identify the thymic branch.
Insecting the thymus tissue from the phrenic nerve will be done very carefully to prevent its injury.
Final diagnosis of this lesion was a time excess.
Remnants of pericardial fat should be removed as well.
Then we started the second stage of the surgery to perform a wedge resection of the pulmonary nodule in the right lung.
After removal of some adhesions between the lung and chest wall, two staplers reloads were needed to perform this wedge section.
Final diagnosis of this lesion was fibrous tissue hyperplasia.
